Amsterdam.intro: Amsterdam is the capital and largest city of the Netherlands.  It has a population of 933,680 in June 2024 within the city proper, 1,457,018 in the urban area and 2,480,394 in the metropolitan area.

Stuttgart.intro: Stuttgart is the capital and largest city of the German state of Baden-Württemberg.  It is located on the Neckar river in a fertile valley known as the Stuttgarter Kessel and lies an hour from the Swabian Jura and the Black Forest.

Brussels.intro: Brussels, officially the Brussels-Capital Region, is a region of Belgium comprising 19 municipalities, including the City of Brussels, which is the capital of Belgium.  The Brussels-Capital Region is located in the central portion of the country.

Prague.intro: Prague is the capital and largest city of the Czech Republic and the historical capital of Bohemia.  Prague, located on the Vltava River, has a population of about 1.

Maastricht.intro: Maastricht is a city and a municipality in the southeastern Netherlands.  It is the capital and largest city of the province of Limburg.

The Hague.intro: The Hague is the capital city of the South Holland province of the Netherlands.  With a population of over half a million, it is the third-largest city in the Netherlands.

Torrevieja.intro: Torrevieja is a Mediterranean-seaside city and municipality on the Costa Blanca, in the province of Alicante, Valencian Community, in southeastern Spain.  The city is in one of the only Spanish-speaking areas of the Valencian Community.

Delft.intro: Delft is a city and municipality in the province of South Holland, Netherlands.  It is located between Rotterdam, to the southeast, and The Hague, to the northwest.

Santiago de Compostela.intro: Santiago de Compostela, alternatively abbreviated to Santiago or Compostela, is the capital of the autonomous community of Galicia, Spain.  It is located in the province of A Coruña, in the northwest of the Iberian Peninsula.

Seville.intro: Seville is the capital and largest city of the Spanish autonomous community of Andalusia and the province of Seville.  It is situated on the lower reaches of the River Guadalquivir, in the southwest of the Iberian Peninsula.

Vigo.intro: Vigo is a city and municipality in Spain, in the autonomous community of Galicia in the province of Pontevedra, and the capital of the comarca of Vigo.  Located in the northwest of the Iberian Peninsula, it sits on the southern shore of an inlet of the Atlantic Ocean, the Ria de Vigo, the southernmost of the Rías Baixas.

Basel.intro: Basel, also known as Basle, is a city in northwestern Switzerland on the river Rhine.  Basel is Switzerland's third-most-populous city, with 177,595 inhabitants within the city municipality limits.

A Coruna.intro: A Coruña is a city and municipality in Galicia, Spain.  With a population of 249,255, it is the 2nd-largest city in Galicia behind Vigo.

Budva.intro: Budva is a town in the Coastal region of Montenegro.  It had 17,479 inhabitants as of 2023, and is the centre of Budva Municipality.

Alanya.intro: Alanya, formerly Alaiye, is a beach resort city, a municipality and district of Antalya Province, Turkey.  It is on the southern coast of Turkey, in the country's Mediterranean Region, 133 kilometres (83 mi) east of the city of Antalya.

Haarlem.intro: Haarlem is a city and municipality in the Netherlands.  It is the capital of the province of North Holland.

Mulhouse.intro: Mulhouse is a French city of the European Collectivity of Alsace.  It is near the border with Switzerland and Germany.

Alicante.intro: Alicante or Alacant is a city and municipality in the Valencian Community, Spain.  It is the capital of the province of Alicante and a historic Mediterranean port.

Lisbon.intro: Lisbon is the capital and most populous city of Portugal, with an estimated population of 575,739 as of 2024, within its administrative limits and 3,028,000 within the metropolis, as of 2025.  Lisbon is mainland Europe's westernmost capital city, and the only one along the Atlantic coast, the others being on islands.

Elche.intro: Elche or Elx is a city and municipality of Spain, belonging to the province of Alicante, in the Valencian Community.  With of 242,317 inhabitants as of 2024, it is the 3rd-most populated municipality in the region.

Kammerstein.intro: Kammerstein is a municipality in the district of Roth in Bavaria in Germany.

Nantes.intro: Nantes is a city in the Loire-Atlantique department of France on the Loire, 50 km (31 mi) from the Atlantic coast.  The city is the sixth largest in France, with a population of 327,734 in Nantes proper (2023) and a metropolitan area of over 1 million inhabitants (2022).

Bouguenais.intro: Bouguenais is a commune in the Loire-Atlantique department in western France near Nantes.

Angers.intro: Angers is a city in western France, about 300 km (190 mi) southwest of Paris.  It is the prefecture of the Maine-et-Loire department and was the capital of the province of Anjou until the French Revolution.

Istanbul.intro: Istanbul is the largest city in Turkey, constituting the country's economic, cultural, and historical center.  With a population of over 15 million, it is home to 18% of the population of Turkey.

Madrid.intro: Madrid is the capital and most populous city of Spain.  It had a population of over 3.

Budapest.intro: Budapest is the capital and most populous city of Hungary.  It is Hungary's primate city with 1.

Mrčevac.intro: Mrčevac is a village in the municipality of Tivat, Montenegro.

Dusseldorf.intro: Düsseldorf is the capital city of North Rhine-Westphalia, the most populous state of Germany.  It is the second-largest city in the state after Cologne and the sixth-largest city in Germany, with a 2024 population of 618,685.

Leinfelden-Echterdingen.intro: Leinfelden-Echterdingen is a town in the district of Esslingen, in Baden-Württemberg, Germany.  It is located approximately 10 km south of Stuttgart, near the Stuttgart Airport and directly adjacent to the newly built Stuttgart Trade Fair.

Strasbourg.intro: Strasbourg is a prefecture and commune, and the largest city of the Grand Est region of northeastern France, in the historic region of Alsace.  It is the prefecture of the Bas-Rhin department and the official seat of the European Parliament.

A Coruña.intro: A Coruña is a city and municipality in Galicia, Spain.  With a population of 249,255, it is the 2nd-largest city in Galicia behind Vigo.

Antalya.intro: Antalya is one of the largest cities in Turkey and the capital of Antalya Province, the fifth-most populous province.  Recognized as the "capital of tourism" in Turkey and a pivotal part of the Turkish Riviera, Antalya sits on Anatolia's southwest coast, flanked by the Taurus Mountains.

Düsseldorf.intro: Düsseldorf is the capital city of North Rhine-Westphalia, the most populous state of Germany.  It is the second-largest city in the state after Cologne and the sixth-largest city in Germany, with a 2024 population of 618,685.

Offenburg.intro: Offenburg is a city in the state of Baden-Württemberg, in south-western Germany.  With nearly 60,000 inhabitants (2019), it is the largest city and the administrative capital of the Ortenaukreis.

Freiburg im Breisgau.intro: Freiburg im Breisgau or simply Freiburg, is the fourth-largest city in the German state of Baden-Württemberg after Stuttgart, Mannheim and Karlsruhe.  Its built-up area has a population of about 355,000 (2021), while the greater Freiburg metropolitan area ("Einzugsgebiet") has about 660,000 (2018).
